Lost World Of Tambun Ipoh

Make your Malaysia trip extra memorable at Sunway Lost World of Tambun Amusement and Water Park! Enter Malaysia's premier family destination and explore its themed areas and over 80 attractions. Begin your trip by going to the Lost World Water Park, featuring the country's biggest wave pool, Jungle Wave Bay, and the longest man-made river, Adventure River. Experience a thrilling ride on the two water slides in the park, the Cliff Racer and Tube Raider! Don't miss out on the Kids Explorabay with your little ones.
88 Attractions 10 Parks: The park is cocooned by lush tropical jungle, natural hot springs, and 400 million-year-old limestone hills.
123 Animal Species: The park is home to 123 different species of animals.
Natural Hot Springs: The park is the only theme park in Southeast Asia with natural hot springs.
Water Park, Amusement Park, Petting Zoo, Tiger Valley, Tin Valley, Hot Springs & Spa: These are some of the main attractions within the park.
Hotspring Night Park: Experience the magical ambiance of the park as it transforms into a haven of relaxation and tranquility at night.

Discover a full day of adventure at The Lost World of Tambun. Cool off at the Water Park, enjoy thrilling rides at the Amusement Park, and explore Ipoh’s tin mining heritage at Tin Valley. Get close to majestic Siberian tigers during feeding time, meet friendly animals at the Petting Zoo, and boost your adrenaline at the Adventure Park with fun, team-based activities suitable for all ages.
Operation Hours: Please kindly visit Lost World Of Tabun official website for any updates of operation hours.

Restrictions: Children 12 years of age and below must be accompanied by a competent person aged 18 years and above.
Dresscode: Guests are encouraged to wear appropriate swimming attire to enjoy the pools at all times for safety and hygiene purposes.
Dresscode: Appropriate swimming attire with Lycra or Nylon material are encouraged (these are the non-absorbent material which is suitable for swimming).
Dresscode: Please comply with the dress code displayed at the relevant areas of the park, including those relating to swimwear.
Accessibility: Baby strollers, wheelchairs and push cars are available for rent inside the park.

Maha Tower

Indulge in the sky-high delights of the Maha Tower as you set sail on an unforgettable seaside adventure along the coast of the Andaman Sea. Poised to be the leading leisure and business hub in Langkawi, the tower features a transcendent sky-high experience that elevates, excites, and awakens your senses. A quintessential Langkawian experience not to be missed.
Glimpse of spectacular sunset
Enjoy a 360° view of Andaman Sea and city of Kuah from 102meter top
Offers transcendent sky-high experience

Indulge in a world-class leisure experience on the Maha Tower esplanade, where you’ll find a curated collection of wonders that cater to everything you need, all in one place. Blending the best of retail with sky-high splendour, the Maha Tower experience is specially curated to awaken your senses, boasting a unique charm that you can’t get elsewhere.The tower’s unique tilted viewing window also allows visitors to “hover” over the panoramic views of the bustling crowds dotted around the esplanade, coupled with the magical sparkle of the vast Andaman Sea.More than a world-class destination for stunning vistas, the Maha Tower is also a hub for elevated leisure experiences that keep you engaged and inspired with a curated selection of activities.

Museum Of Illusions Kuala Lumpur

Take a peek inside of the Museum of Illusions Kuala Lumpur!Welcome to the fascinating world of illusions! Get entertained, confused and completely blown away, experience the unimaginable and learn all about how our wonderful mind works. You’re up for an amazing experience because nothing is what is seems to be at the Museum of Illusions Kuala Lumpur.
"Best place for edutainment in Kuala Lumpur "
Deceive your eyes as you explore the wide collection of hologram installations and tricks
Grab your camera and enjoy taking pictures with various optical illusion displays
Perfect for family and friends, the museum houses many fun-filled activities for everyone
You won't believe your eyes!
The Museum of Illusions is a 21st-century museum offering a unique experience that tricks the mind, challenges the vision and makes you question all of your senses.
All exhibits are based on science, mathematics, psychology and art allowing you to learn a lot about vision and the human brain, but in a fun and interactive way!
Restriction:  While photography is encouraged, using flash photography or tripods might be restricted in certain areas.
Restriction: Visitors with mobility limitations or who are pregnant should exercise caution and navigate the museum at their own pace.
Restriction:  Be aware that eating and drinking are not permitted inside the exhibition halls.
This allows you to inquire about the specific accessibility features of the museum on the day of your visit and discuss any accommodations you might need.

PD Ostrich Show Farm

PD Ostrich Show Farm is a top attraction in Port Dickson, with ostriches as the star but there are lots of other farm and pet animals, including rabbits, ponies, donkeys, birds, snakes.The farm offers guided tours that take visitors through the various stages of ostrich breeding and farming. Visitors can learn about the different types of ostriches, their characteristics, and their life cycle. There are also opportunities to feed and pet the ostriches, as well as take photos with them.Apart from ostriches, the farm also features other farm animals such as rabbits, goats, and horses. There are various shows and activities available for visitors, including ostrich races, egg painting demonstrations, and a bird show featuring different species of birds.
Petting farm with ostriches, rabbits and other pets & farm animals. Lots of fruit trees
Planet Jurassic is a dinosaur park with some sculptures which can also move and sound.
Planet Jurassic is a dinosaur park included in this combo package are Carnivorous Plant Garden and The Country Cat Cafe

Splash Out Langkawi Water Theme Park Marketplace

Escape from the hustle and bustle of the city, take your loved ones to the much-celebrated Splash Out Langkawi, and spend your weekend cooling down with a range of water activities.Slide down the colors of the Rainbow Rider for thrills and spills, or get drenched at Big Splash Kingdom. Ride the gut-wrenching Perfect Storm to show off your bravery and be amazed by the rough ride that will keep you spinning toward the ground. For something more relaxing, casually float on the River of Life and feel the gentle current carry your stresses away.
Location: The waterpark is located in Kedah Langkawi, around 10 minutes drive from the main ferry terminal.
Attractions: The park features 12 water attractions (River of Life, Big Splash Kingdom, Perfect Storm).
The park is suitable for all ages, with several sections great for younger kids.
Splash Kingdom is a water play area for children 12 years old and below.
Many of the bigger slides and activities have a minimum height requirement of 120cm.

Ditch the city heat and cool down with a fun-filled water adventure at Splash Out Langkawi! Take on the twists and turns of the Rainbow Rider, get drenched at Big Splash Kingdom, or conquer the thrilling Perfect Storm. Feeling relaxed? Float down the calming River of Life. Splash Out Langkawi offers something for everyone - thrills, chills, and a perfect weekend escape!
Food/Drinks: Outside options may be restricted, check park policy.
Lost & Found: Report lost items to staff.
Age Limits: Some items may have age restrictions.

SplashMania at Gamuda Cove, Selangor

Where nature meets fun, and where you can spend the entire day splashing around under the sun! Experience the thrills and spills of a one-of-a-kind waterpark in SplashMania. Home to 39 exhilarating slides and attractions that cater to families, students, young working adults and thrill seekers of all kinds, this waterpark is located in Gamuda Cove, Selangor. Close proximity to KLIA and 30mins from KL City Centre.
SplashMania at Gamuda Cove in Selangor is a unique waterpark where nature meets fun.
It features first-of-its-kind attractions, award-winning water slides, and entertainment offerings.
Top Attractions: Wild Rush, Titanic Experience, Amazonia, Duf Mania, Ravage River, Zoomi's Runway, Sky Leap, Shaka Waka and Plung Swoosh.

Gear up for thrills and spills at SplashMania, Gamuda Cove's water wonderland! This action-packed park promises a day of drenched delight for all ages. With 39 exhilarating slides and attractions, there's something for every level of thrill-seeker.For the adrenaline junkies, heart-pounding rides like The Shaka Waka, a colossal looping water coaster, and The Wild Rush, a multi-rider raft adventure, promise an unforgettable rush. Take a plunge down the gravity-defying slides or race your friends on the twisting tubes.For a more relaxing experience, meander along the lazy river or unwind in the wave pool. Families can create lasting memories splashing around in the dedicated children's area, featuring age-appropriate slides, water jets, and fountains.The fun doesn't stop at the water! Take a break from the slides and challenge your friends to a game of mini golf or test your skills at the arcade. When hunger strikes, a variety of restaurants and cafes are available to satisfy your cravings.Soak up the sun, cool off in the refreshing water, and create a splash at SplashMania! This ultimate water park adventure is a perfect escape for a fun-filled day trip with family and friends.
Accessibility: Stroller and wheelchairs. These amenities are available to rent during your visit.
Accessibility: Place for mother to feeds their babies.  Have clear signages at the venue indicating where the area is made available.
Weather: For safety reasons, we must promptly evacuate the pool during heavy rain accompanied by lightning and thunderstorms.
Restriction: Those under 13 must always be accompanied by an adult.
Restriction: SplashMania visitors cannot bring any dangerous or illegal items into the premises.
What’s included: Standard Admission

Terms & Conditions: Tickets are valid for a fixed date only Multiple vouchers can be redeemed together in a single transaction. Each voucher is valid for one (1) time redemption only. The voucher is valid only on the same day of the visit. Any unused voucher after its validity period will be forfeited and not refunded. The voucher is non-transferable, non-refundable, and cannot be exchanged for cash or any other products or merchandise in the waterpark. If the voucher value in a single transaction is insufficient to cover the total cost, the voucher holder must pay the remaining balance. If the voucher value exceeds the total cost, any remaining amount will be forfeited and not refunded. SplashMania management reserves the right to vary and amend the terms and conditions at any time without prior notice. Guests must follow the general park rules and regulations for SplashMania, Gamuda Cove. FREE park admission applies only to children aged below 3 years with a paying adult. Valid documents such as NRIC, MyKid, I-Card, Passport, or Multiple Entry Visa will be required for verification purposes prior to park admission. Guests must adhere to all safety rules and regulations of the slides and rides irrespective of the ticket type. Guests with Disabilities (OKU): Guests with a disability certificate. Guests below the age of 12 must be accompanied at all times by an adult aged 18 and above. Outside food and drinks are strictly not allowed. Glass or any sharp or dangerous objects are strictly prohibited. No personal surfboards, body boards, or tubes are allowed. Guests are solely responsible for their own belongings. No refunds for inclement weather. No exchange, refund, replacement, and/or extension will be made under any circumstance(s). No cancellation will be accepted upon confirmation of purchase. Only approved swimwear is allowed. No exposed zippers, buckles, or rivets. Guests must use a tube or sliding mat to go on certain slides and attractions. Please read all ride signage and adhere accordingly. Queue up and wait for your turn to get onto slides or rides. Most rides have height and weight restrictions. Please read all ride restriction signage and adhere accordingly. CAUTION: Guests with any existing medical health condition or who have self-imposed restrictions on their physical activity, or who are overweight for their body type or under “doctor’s care” should not go on any rides or attractions. CAUTION: Certain attractions contain bright lighting effects that flash or strobe. Guests who are sensitive to these conditions or have a history of photosensitive epilepsy should avoid these attractions. Locker rental is available at your convenience. Please do not leave valuables unattended. Fighting and abusive language are strictly not permitted in SplashMania. Gamuda Land Leisure’s Management (“Management”) reserves the right to stop any picture-taking or video recording and confiscate any materials if deemed inappropriate. The Management will not be liable for any loss, injuries, or death. The Management reserves the right to deny admission or request a guest to leave the park due to infraction of the rules or if caught in unsafe, illegal, or offensive behavior. The Management reserves the right to change the operation hours, close any part of the park, restrict the number of visitors, cancel, or amend any activities without prior notice.
Cancellation Policy: No cancellation for this activity as per vendor policy

Sunway Lagoon Theme Park

Experience never-ending fun at the award-winning water park in Malaysia — Sunway Lagoon! Explore six adventure zones within the park featuring thrilling rides and attractions. Come to the Amusement Park for cowboy rollercoaster action or view the world upside down at the 360-degree pirate ship. Get soaking wet at the Sunway Lagoon's Water Park, where you can surf giant waves at Surf Beach or slide down the vuvuzela, the world's highest and largest water ride. Set foot in Asia's first Nickelodeon-themed park, Nickelodeon Lost Lagoon, and go on splashy adventures with your favorite characters.
Multi-Park Extravaganza: Sunway Lagoon is a multi-park extravaganza, with 80 attractions spread over 360,000 square meters.
Exciting Rides: There are plenty of fun rides that cater to both children and adults.
Bungee Jumping: Sunway Lagoon offers thrilling facilities like the chance to go bungee jumping.
Kayaking and Archery: You can also learn archery or go kayaking at Sunway Lagoon.
Flying Fox: Experience the thrill of the Flying Fox at Sunway Lagoon.

Get ready for an action-packed day at Sunway Lagoon with something for everyone. Enjoy thrilling rides at the Amusement Park, cool off at the Water Park with high-speed slides and wave pools, and get close to over 150 animal species at the Wildlife Park. Brave the spine-chilling Scream Park, splash into Asia’s first Nickelodeon-themed land, and fuel your adrenaline at X Park with extreme activities like Flying Fox, bungee jumping, go-karting, and ATV rides.
Restrictions: Please also kindly visit Sunway Lagoon Theme Park official website for height requirements for the rides.
Dresscode: Appropriate swimwear is encouraged at the park at all times for safety and hygiene purposes.
Wheelchairs are available inside the park.
What’s included: Admission to Sunway Lagoon Theme Park (Water Park, Amusement Park, Wildlife Park, Extreme Park, Scream Park & Nickelodeon Lost Lagoon)

Terms & Conditions: Admission Ticket valid on visit date Non-Malaysian is for International / Passport holders. Re-entry to the park is not allowed. Wristband tags will be removed upon exit. Wristband tags are valid for entry only on the date of visit. Eligibility Child Rate: Ages 12 years old and below or Senior aged 60 years old and above. Child rate applies to children above 90 cm in height or aged 12 years old and below (based on year of birth). Child rate is based on height (90 cm and above) and not by age 4 years old. Free Admission: Children below 90 cm in height. Children under 12 years old must be accompanied by an adult. All ages are determined based on year of birth. Attraction Access IC and Passport authentication is required at the ticketing counter. Ticket confirmation applies for Adult, Child, and Senior guests. The cashier reserves the right to reject redemption if the information provided is unclear or does not match. If the ticket name differs from the IC/Passport, the venue reserves the right to refuse admission without refund. Additional Information Penalty Fees: Upgrade MyKad Child to MyKad Adult: RM 50 per person Upgrade MyKad to Non-MyKad: RM 100 per person All transactions in the park are via cash or E-load. E-load can be topped up at the Ticketing Counter. Unused E-load balance can be refunded on the same day before 7 PM at the Ticketing Counter. When using waterslides, the following are not permitted: jewelry, T-shirts or swimwear with metal decorations, cut-off jeans, and glasses. Slides are used at guests’ own risk. Sunway Lagoon encourages guests to secure valuables. The park is not responsible for lost or stolen items. Guests are responsible for their own belongings. Dangerous items such as bottles, firecrackers, lasers, and other items that may disturb other guests are prohibited inside the venue. Important Notes Sunway Lagoon reserves the right to carry out renovations or upgrading works when necessary. Management reserves the right to change operating hours, close the park, rides, or attractions due to maintenance, weather, special events, or unforeseen circumstances without prior notice. Insurance coverage is applicable (terms and conditions apply). General Park Rules of Sunway Lagoon apply, refer to "http://sunwaylagoon.com/park-rules"
Cancellation Policy: Free cancellation if cancelled till the selected date.

Melaka Menara Taming Sari Tower

A 360° viewing tower that provides a breath-taking view form a height of 80 meters in the air. The 7-minutes ride enables you to view spectacular and panoramic sights of Melaka. UNESCO World Heritage City such as St Paul’s Hill, Independence Memorial Building, Samudra Museum (Flor De La Mar), Melaka River, Dataran Pahlawan, Selat Melaka Mosque, Pulau Besar and the Straits of Melaka.While enjoying the exhilarating sightseeing in the air, the ride also enables you to witness the rapid development taking place in Melaka.With an overall tower height of 110 meters from the ground to the top and built using Swiss technology, the revolving glass cabin has a passenger capacity of 66 people per viewing session.
A 360° viewing tower that provides a breath-taking view form a height of 80 meters in the air.
Menara Taming Sari in Melaka is a popular attraction that offers a spectacular and panoramic view of the Historical Malacca City from a height of 80 meters.
With an overall tower height of 110 meters from the ground to the top and built using Swiss technology, the revolving glass cabin can accommodate 66 passengers.

The tower is equipped with a revolving viewing deck that rotates 360 degrees, allowing visitors to enjoy breathtaking views of Malacca City from different angles. The ride lasts for about 7 minutes and gives visitors a bird's eye view of the city's historical landmarks and attractions, including St. Paul's Hill, Independence Memorial Building, and the Straits of Malacca.

Apart from the viewing deck, Menara Taming Sari also has a souvenir shop located on the ground floor, where visitors can purchase unique gifts and souvenirs. The tower is also equipped with modern facilities such as elevators and air conditioning, ensuring a comfortable and enjoyable experience for visitors.

Tech Dome Penang

Tech Dome Penang is an initiative by the Penang State Government to create a hub for technology learning and exchange of ideas. It will be a vehicle for improving scientific literacy and technology ability in Malaysia. Located at the geodesic dome of KOMTAR just adjacent to the George Town UNESCO World Heritage Site in Penang, visitors will discover the exciting world of technology and how they work by exploring the world-class exhibits in its galleries. Regular programs will be conducted for schools, colleges and families that are specially designed to cultivate the spirit of inquiry and teach children and teenagers the skills required to thrive in a high-tech, knowledge-based future.
A not-for-profit science and technology center, offers an exciting array of exhibits and experiences for visitors.
STEM Showcase 2024: Tech Dome Penang is proud to present the much-anticipated event of the year – the STEM Showcase.
Mini Penang: Within the museum, you’ll find Mini Penang, a charming display where miniature bears are dressed and placed in small sets.
Photo Opportunities: Capture memorable moments with life-sized teddy bears in whimsical settings.
Main Galleries

- Life Tech takes a walk through the sciences on how our body works and technologies that maintain human health. From nutrients to diagnostic systems, to technologies in an operating theatre, the visitor will gain an appreciation of the many levels, technologies and people that work together to keep people healthy!

- Forces & Motion lets you experience the physical branch of science. From gravity to momentum, to kinetics and dynamics, visitors will be able to learn Newton's laws of motion through a full-body experience!

- Electromagnetism tells the love story between electricity and magnetism. Visitors will learn about the flow of electrons, experience magnetic attraction and repulsion, and see magnetic fields with their very own eyes. Be careful as you may see sparks fly!

- Robotics pulls a robot apart into its various components for you to appreciate how they function. Visitors will have the chance to play and tinker with robots and understand how robots think, sense and act.

- Information Technology celebrates the humble sand and its place in modern technology. Integrated circuits together with other electronic components are now a cornerstone of modern day devices and appliances. Visitors will learn how electronic components are made and how they "talk" with each other to perform functions in our electronic devices.

- Optics will demonstrate the unique characteristics of different types of light. Visitors will learn how different types of light can be used, experience how each light influences human perception, and understand the chemistry of creating light and why we use energy-efficient lighting.
Operation Hours: Please kindly visit Tech Dome Penang official website for any updates of operation hours.
Weather: Indoor attraction with no wet weather restrictions.
Dresscode: Casual wear recommended.
Accessibility: Tech Dome Penang is wheelchair friendly with a wheelchair friendly toilet and elevators to bring guests up to the Mezzanine floor.

The Bentong Farm

The Bentong Farm is an ideal destination for a day trip with the family, located just an hour's drive from Kuala Lumpur. Surrounded by lush forests, green fields, and fruit orchards, it provides a refreshing escape from the hustle and bustle of the city.At The Bentong Farm, visitors can enjoy a variety of activities, including interacting with exotic animals such as the 'World's Smallest Horse', South American alpacas, snakes, tortoises, marmosets, tropical birds, and more. Visitors can also handle and feed some of the animals under the supervision of the staff, providing a unique and personalized experience.Children can even go horseback riding, adding an extra touch of adventure to their visit. The farm's serene environment offers a chance to relax and enjoy the natural beauty of the countryside, while the range of activities available ensures that there is something for everyone to enjoy.
The Bentong Farm, located just 8 minutes from Bentong town and an hour’s drive from Kuala Lumpur, is a magnificent display of nature and beautiful landscapes.
The farm is spread across 27 acres of beautifully landscaped areas with magnificent waterfalls, ponds, and streams, creating a sense of being one with nature.
Animal Interactions: The farm is home to over 100 species of tamed yet exotic creatures.
Educational Programs: The farm provides a suitable and safe place for visitors from all walks of life and ages to learn more about nature, animals, and plants.
The farm has won the award as the ‘Best Agro Tourism Destination’ by MATA and Santai Travel and is also accredited by Malaysia Tourism Quality Assurance (MyTQA)

magine a haven of lush greenery where rows of vegetables flourish, fruit trees laden with seasonal bounty, and friendly farm animals await your visit. The Bentong Farm allows visitors to get up close and personal with a variety of animals, from gentle goats and fluffy sheep to clucking chickens and playful rabbits. You might even have the opportunity to feed them or learn about their care from the farm's experienced staff.The educational aspect goes beyond simple observation. The Bentong Farm offers interactive workshops and demonstrations that delve into the world of agriculture. You might learn about soil management, planting techniques, harvesting practices, and the importance of sustainable farming methods.  These activities are not only informative but also a fun way to engage with the agricultural process firsthand.

The Top Penang

The Top Penang is a popular tourist destination located in the heart of Georgetown, Penang, Malaysia. It is a multi-attraction complex that offers a wide range of entertainment and leisure activities for visitors of all ages.Some of the top attractions at The Top Penang include:1. Rainbow Skywalk: This is a glass walkway that extends out from the 68th floor of the Komtar tower, offering panoramic views of Georgetown and the surrounding area.2. Observatory Deck: This is an indoor observation deck located on the 65th floor of the Komtar tower, offering 360-degree views of the city and its landmarks.3. Jurassic Research Center: This is an interactive dinosaur exhibit that features life-size replicas of various prehistoric creatures, as well as interactive displays that teach visitors about the history and behavior of dinosaurs.4. Tech Dome: This is a science and technology center that features a range of exhibits and activities, including interactive displays, workshops, and hands-on experiments.5. Indoor Theme Park: The indoor theme park features a variety of rides and attractions, including roller coasters, bumper cars, and carnival games.In addition to these attractions, The Top Penang also offers a range of food and beverage options, as well as shopping and entertainment venues.In conclusion, The Top Penang is a must-visit destination for anyone looking for fun and entertainment in Georgetown. It is open daily from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM, and admission fees apply for each attraction.
The Top Penang is a popular tourist destination located in the heart of Georgetown, Penang, Malaysia.
It is a multi-attraction complex that offers a wide range of entertainment and leisure activities for visitors of all ages.
The TOP are categorised into two groups called "Avenue of Adventures" and "Window of The Top."
It is a combo of several attractions including the Tech Dome Penang, Rainbow Skywalk, Indoor Observatory Deck and many more.
Attractions:

- Jurassic Research Center: Travel back in time to when dinosaurs ruled the Earth and uncover the mysteries of the planet’s prehistoric past. The Jurassic Research Center brings over 200 dinosaurs back to life (with the help of the latest animatronics technology) to create a hands-on interactive activity center of epic proportions.  Meet and frolic with these creatures from millions of years ago! Find out how they used to roam the Earth! Discover the enthralling realm of dinosaurs first-hand!

Jurassic Research Center takes you through a truly education journey for both kids and adults where they can experience the life size dinosaurs in their habitat. Come see the lost world that was once alive million years ago through the eye of science!

- Rainbow Skywalk: Ever wonder what a bird’s eye view of Penang is like? The Rainbow Skywalk and Observatory Deck offer stunning seamless views of George Town and beyond. Located at George Town’s highest point, the open-air Rainbow Skywalk is at Level 68, on the rooftop of Komtar tower, offering visitors the thrilling experience of walking on air 816 feet above sea level. Meanwhile, the air-conditioned Observatory Deck at Level 65 also features a glass walkway that lets you watch the world go by below.
Also on the rooftop, Top View restaurant & lounge @ 68th offers great tapas food and cocktails as well as a great view. Enjoy the feeling of being on the top of the world.

- Top Boutique Aquarium: Get up close and personal with marine flora and fauna as you go on a journey of discovery at Penang Boutique Aquarium! Home to a myriad of marine life, it promises to offer a wealth of amazement and knowledge for all ages. Learn more about the underwater world and find out how we can all help to conserve and protect our aquatic friends and their homes here.

- Penang State Gallery: Penang has a long and colorful heritage. Since its humble beginnings as the first British Straits Settlement in the late 18th century, George Town has undergone a tremendous transformation. From a swampy frontier to a bustling trading post to a thriving cosmopolitan city that is world-renowned for its living history, the streets of Penang are filled with vibrant nuances just waiting to be discovered by intrepid adventurers. Chock full of information, the Penang State Gallery is a one-stop center for you to learn about anything and everything “Penang” as well as purchasing special souvenirs unique to the state

- Durian The King of Fruits: Find out why the durian is the indisputable king of fruits in Southeast Asia at this center that showcases everything you need to know about the fruit – from its history to its growing process. There are plenty of fun and interesting activities to try and durian-related products to take home! Get up close and personal with all the different members of this thorny, pungent-smelling fruit and taste it in its various forms… if you dare!

Tropical Fruit Farm Penang

Welcome to Penang Tropical Fruit Farm. We have South East Asia’s largest collection of fruits with an active area that spans a sprawling 25 acres located in the North West of Penang, Malaysia. With over 200 species of edible fruits identified, we aim to promote the amazing flora of Malaysia through education and information.Tropical Fruit Farm is situated about 800ft above sea level on the hilly terrains of Teluk Bahang in Penang. It is an ideal location for cultivating many types of tropical and sub-tropical fruit trees. The orchard was developed back in 1993 with a few objectives in mind.Firstly, to conserve rare and exotic fruit trees. Secondly, to educate and promote to the locals as well as foreigners of the gift that our land is blessed with. Lastly, to play a positive role towards agriculture and eco/agro tourism amid the intensive development in our state’s rural areas.Over the years, the orchard has acquired many rare and exotic fruit trees from the region as well as from far-flung corners of the globe such as Central and South America, Central Africa, India, the Middle East, the Caribbean, the Pacific Islands etc. Today the orchard boasts of a collection of over 200 different species and the list is still growing.The farm endeavours to use only organic fertilisers. Some of which are processed in the farm using fruit waste collected from around the farm. Chemicals are only used when absolutely necessary. In the farm, our aim is to work towards a cleaner environment for the future.Apart from viewing the fruits, you will be able to enjoy the varieties of fruits (minimum 15 types!) when you join one of our Guided Farm Tours. The orchard also prepares light snacks, juices and a specially prepared buffet spread for pre-booked groups.

Underwater World Langkawi

Say hello to the lovely penguins and multicolored tropical fish at a water world wonderland in Langkawi, one of the biggest aquariums in Southeast Asia. With more than 5,000 species of animals, including fish, birds, and mammals, the aquarium won't leave you with a moment of boredom!Comfortably stroll through the Marine Life Area, Rainforest Area, and Sub-Antarctic Area. These three zones make up the main areas of Underwater World, and each of them features different exciting animals.UWL Operation Hours for Chinese New Year CelebrationDate : 17 - 18 February 2026Operation Hours: 9.30 am - 9.30 pm
Underwater World Langkawi continues to captivate visitors with its unique blend of knowledge, education, and entertainment.
The Penguinarium: Located in the Sub-Antarctic section, the Penguinarium steals the show.
It stands as the leading facility in Malaysia for breeding jumpy Rockhopper penguins in captivity.
Marmosets in the Rainforest Section: The rainforest area hosts the world’s smallest species of monkeys, the marmosets.
Underwater World Langkawi is not just an aquarium; it’s an immersive experience where adventure meets education.

The concept and theme of Underwater World Langkawi are geared towards Knowledge, Education and Entertainment. It is built to raise awareness on the importance of conserving our precious aquatic life forms, thus creating understanding of the deep and an inseparable bond between man and nature. Discover the wonders of Underwater World Langkawi, where each section offers a unique and captivating experience. Marvel at the Arapaima in the Preview Tank, explore the River Ecosystem with its diverse freshwater fish and reptiles, and stroll through the Tropical Rainforest aviary with Flamingoes, Macaws, and Marmosets. Enjoy the playful South American Fur Seals and African Penguins in the Temperate Section, and meet the Rockhopper Penguins in the Sub-Antarctic Section. Dive into the Hexagonal Tank's “Sunken Ship” theme, venture through the 15-meter Tunnel Tank surrounded by marine life, and relax by the outdoor Koi Pond. Discover unusual sea creatures in the Invertebrate Section and the vibrant Coral Reef Section, and be cautious around the venomous Moray Eels and Lionfish. Each section provides an educational and thrilling experience, offering a deeper understanding of diverse aquatic life and ecosystems.
Accessibility: Children must be accompanied by an adult guardian at all times

Wet World Water Park Shah Alam

Wet World, Malaysia largest chain of waterparks, priding itself as a value for money and family oriented park. Over 13 activities and water slides for you to try. Some are slow, some are fast, some are exciting and some are fun. Wet World Water Park Shah Alam is a family-friendly attraction that offers a variety of water-based activities and rides. The water park features a range of attractions, including water slides, wave pools, lazy rivers, and children's play areas. Some of the most popular rides include: 1. The Big Splash: This is the park's flagship ride, a high-speed water slide that sends riders plummeting down a steep slope and into a pool of water. 2. The Pirate's Cove: This is a play area for children, featuring a variety of interactive water features and small slides. 3. The Wave Pool: This is a large pool that creates waves that simulate the ocean, allowing visitors to swim and play in the waves. 4. In addition to the water attractions, the park also offers a range of facilities, including food and beverage outlets, locker rentals, and shower and changing facilities. Wet World Water Park Shah Alam is a popular destination for families and groups of friends looking to cool off and have some fun in the water.

Wonderfood Museum Penang

Penang Wonderfood Museum presents a kitsch, kooky and in-depth look at the city’s unique food culture with larger-than-life displays of Malaysian dishes. Set within a 1940s colonial building in Georgetown, the museum is divided into three galleries - Info Zone, Wow Zone, and Educational Zone. All of the hyper-realistic models are handcrafted by the museum’s owner, Sean Lao who uses Japanese food replication techniques.The museum features over 100 exhibits of local dishes, ranging from street food to traditional dishes, presented in a realistic and interactive way. The exhibits are arranged in different sections, each showcasing a particular aspect of Penang's food culture. For example, one section displays the ingredients and spices commonly used in Penang cuisine, while another section features the various utensils and equipment used in preparing and serving the dishes. Visitors can also learn about the history and cultural significance of each dish, as well as the cooking techniques and recipes involved in preparing them. The museum offers an immersive experience that allows visitors to see, touch, and even smell the exhibits, making it an excellent way to discover and appreciate the unique flavors of Penang. Overall, the Penang Wonderfood Museum is a must-visit destination for foodies and anyone interested in learning more about the culinary culture of Penang. It is open daily from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM and admission fees apply.
The Wonderfood Museum in Penang is a unique museum that features oversized replicas of traditional Malaysian dishes.
Oversized Food Replicas: The museum showcases a variety of food on display, providing you with an in-depth understanding of the foods popular in Asia.
Multi-Cultural Delicacies: Discover interesting delicacies arising from Malaysia’s multi-cultural background at the museum’s three galleries.
Interactive Exhibits: The exhibits at Wonderfood Museum are replicated with the utmost detail and are sure to tempt your appetite.

Wonderfood Museum is a fun place to see and photograph larger-than-life models of Malaysia's national obsession - food, in all its varied and delicious forms. If you have ever seen those ultra-realistic plastic  replicas of dishes outside Japanese restaurants you will know what to expect here except that the models are mostly of Malaysian food and they are huge. The museum showcases Malaysia's unique food culture, drawn from the nation's rich multi-racial diversity. The replicas are beautifully made and if you weren't hungry when you entered the museum you will be by the time you leave.

Zoo Negara

Zoo Negara Malaysia is managed by the Malaysian Zoological Society, a non-governmental organization established to create the first local zoo for Malaysians.Zoo Negara was officially opened on 14th November 1963 and has matured into a well-known zoo all around the world. We have a total of over 5137 specimen from 476 species of mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians and fish.Zoo Negara covers 110 acres of land which is situated only 5km from the city of Kuala Lumpur.Over the years, the zoo has transformed itself to an open concept zoo with over 90% of its animals being kept in spacious exhibits with landscape befitting its nature.
Also known as National Zoo of Malaysia, a popular attraction located in Ulu Klang, Gombak, Selangor.
Officially opened in 14 November 1963 and managed by Malaysian Zoological Society.
Wide Variety of Species: The zoo is home to over 400 species of mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, and fish.
Animal Shows: Sea lions, macaques, and macaws are part of a multi-animal show that is shown twice daily.
Mini Bee Museum: Located at the heart of Zoo Negara, this museum educates visitors about the many types of bees in the country.

Zoo Negara, also known as the National Zoo of Malaysia, is a 110-acre zoological park in Selangor and home to over 5,000 animals from more than 450 species. The zoo features themed zones such as the African Savannah, Malaysian Rainforest, and Reptile Park, where visitors can see animals like tigers, lions, elephants, giraffes, and orangutans. Visitors can also enjoy animal shows, feeding activities, and interactive experiences, while learning about the zoo’s conservation and endangered species breeding programmes.

Restriction: Bringing outside food and drinks into the zoo is not allowed and Pets of any kind are not allowed inside the zoo.

KLIA Ekspres

Express Rail Link (ERL) is the operator of KLIA Ekspres and KLIA Transit train services that connect Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA & KLIA2) and the city. The non-stop 57-km journey on KLIA Ekspres between KL Sentral and KLIA takes only 28 minutes. Passengers can also take a 3-minute ride between KLIA2 and KLIA. The KLIA Transit service stops at three intermediate stations i.e. Bandar Tasik Selatan, Putrajaya & Cyberjaya and Salak Tinggi. ERL has been recognised locally and internationally for consistently providing high standards in the air-rail services industry. ERL was named AirRail Link of the Year at the Global AirRail Awards in 2012, 2014, 2015 and 2016. On the home front, ERL was awarded the Best Operator (Railway) in 2017 by the Land Public Transport Commission of Malaysia (SPAD). To date, ERL has carried more than 90 million passengers.KLIA Ekspres:- Fastest way to the city- Frequent train departures - WiFi onboard - Free porter service on availability - Easy connection from KL Sentral Station to anywhere in the cityAirline Check-in at KL City Air Terminal (KL CAT) in KL Sentral is currently not available
KLIA Ekspres is a non-stop, airport rail link service between Kuala Lumpur Sentral (KL Sentral) and the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) and KLIA2.

Koisaan Cultural Village

The Koisaan Cultural Village is located at Hongkod Koisaan KDCA Complex, Km 8, Penampang Road. The Village represents a living museum to preserve the multi-ethnic Kadazandusun culture. It aims to share the knowledge, history, and cultural heritage of the multi-ethnic Kadazandusun community.The KCV offers you the opportunity to see and experience the culture and lifestyle of how the indigenous ethnic communities of the Kadazandusun used to live in the past.The village features 11 different ethnic cultural houses in one village. They are The Penampang Cultural House, The Tenom Cultural – Lansaran House, The Beaufort Cultural House, The Kota Belud Cultural House, The Kuala Penyu Cultural House, The Keningau Cultural House, The Tambunan Cultural House, The Papar Cultural House, The Ranau Cultural House, The Tuaran Cultural House and The Kudat Cultural Long House
The Koisaan Cultural Village is at Hongkod Koisaan KDCA Complex, preserving Kadazandusun culture.
It's a living museum sharing the history and lifestyle of the Kadazandusun community.
The village has 11 ethnic houses, like The Penampang Cultural House, providing a glimpse into diverse traditions in one place.
Explore the rich heritage of the Kadazandusun community at Koisaan Cultural Village, featuring 11 ethnic houses showcasing their unique traditions and lifestyle with
various kind of cultural activities.

Taman Buaya & Rekreasi Melaka

Experience the unique wildlife of Malaysia at the Melaka Crocodile and Recreation Park, a sprawling 13,000 sq.ft local farm nestled in the historic city of Melaka. Immerse yourself in lush greenery and beautiful vegetation as you explore the farm's diverse facilities. Encounter over 100 crocodiles of four different species in their natural habitat, witnessing trainers interact with these fascinating creatures. The park is also home to various other animals, including emus, tortoises, deers, reptiles, racoons, and more. Explore Miniature Malaysia, featuring smaller replicas of iconic buildings, and cool off in the waterpark—an ideal destination for quality family time!
Encounter a variety of crocodiles up close in Melaka's largest reptile park
Enjoy family-friendly recreational activities and scenic park surroundings
Explore a mini zoo featuring a variety of other animals.

Embark on an adventure at the Melaka Crocodile and Recreation Park, discovering over 100 crocodiles, diverse wildlife, and Miniature Malaysia's iconic replicas. Enjoy family-friendly facilities, wildlife interactions, and a refreshing waterpark for a day filled with fun and exploration.
Do not feed the animals.
Follow instructions from park rangers and show performers.
Supervise children at all times.

Wetland Studios Putrajaya

Wetland Studios Putrajaya is a one-of-a-kind edutainment indoor theme park not to be missed when you visit Putrajaya, Malaysia. Have fun learning about the past, present and how we can shape a better future as future generations to come.KRU Studios Sdn Bhd (KRU) is a multi-award-winning media and entertainment company in Malaysia, and an emerging brand globally.On 5 October 2022, Putrajaya Corporation and KRU signed a collaboration agreement whereby KRU is entrusted to design, market and operate Wetland Studios Putrajaya with the objective to promote the flora and fauna of Putrajaya Wetland Park using modern technology to create an immersive, memorable, entertaining, and rewarding experience for our visitors.
Unite fun and education at Putrajaya's unique indoor theme park.
Shape a sustainable future at Wetland Studios in Malaysia.
Explore past, present, and future through edutainment at Wetland Studios, Putrajaya.
Enjoy scenic walking trails, guided boat tours, and interactive exhibits.

Expect a captivating journey through time and nature, blending education and entertainment at Wetland Studios PutrajayaPutrajaya Wetland Park serves as a sanctuary for a diverse array of wildlife, including hundreds of species of birds, mammals, reptiles, fish, insects, trees, and plants. The park provides vital habitat and food sources for these various organisms, playing a crucial role in supporting their survival. Many of the species found within the park are threatened or endangered, making the conservation efforts at Putrajaya Wetland Park even more crucial. By maintaining a healthy ecosystem with a variety of habitats, the park helps sustain populations of these vulnerable species and contributes to their protection and conservation.
Restriction: You must be at least 18 years old or above as of the time of your ticket purchase.

Night Safari

Retreat into this mysterious animal kingdom and heighten your senses for the nocturnal Ultimate Wildlife Experience. Under the moonlit skies, immerse yourself in a magical kingdom where wild animals roam when they are most naturally active.Meet over 1,000 creatures from 120 species as you embark on an adventure on our tram ride and four walking trails. Start the night on a high as our fiery Thumbuakar tribal performers welcome you into the Park and get set for a truly close encounter with the wild.
Hop on our open-sided tram and journey through 6 of the world’s geographical regions.
Explore our Safari Trails and embark on the Safari Tram Adventure. Your night here is set to be wild!
The night's calling! There’s no better way to experience the ‘dark of the action’ than to embark on one of our multi-sensory activities.
Embark on a wild nocturnal adventure
Activities:
- Wildlife Experiences: Go on a buggy-and-walking adventure in the company of an expert guide who will revel you with insider tales about our animals.
- Presentations: Catch our presentations to witness the amazing talents of our animals and performers.
- Keeper Talks: Coming on a weekend? Be sure to catch our big cats in action during their snack time and watch them devour their meaty treats!
- Unique Dining Experience: Escape the city life and enjoy a night at Night Safari's Tipi tent. Indulge in an evening of gourmet food and amazing wildlife encounters!

- Night Photography E-Guide: Having a challenge with your night shots? Learn how you can create that insta-worthy photo with tips and tricks from 4 of Sony’s Photographers. Download the e-guide and be ready to impress!

- Indian Rhino Feeding at East Lodge Trail: Get up close with these one-horned heavyweights as they feed off your hand under the moonlit sky. Don’t miss out on this great photo moment! This experience is available as an add-on for $10 per food portion.
Operation Hours: Please kindly visit Night Safari's official website for any updates
Dresscode: Casual wear recommended.
Accessibility: Wheelchair, stroller, and electric scooters accessible.

Cameron Highlands and Mossy Forest Half Day Tour

This moss-choked forest was ranked fifth place on Lonely Planet's list of Most Instagrammable Forests In The World and looks straight out of a scene from the Lord Of The Rings or Avatar. Just like its name, it is filled with trees and all sorts of flora covered with layers of moss. Join a tour for a hassle-free, enjoyable visit inclusive of round-trip transfers and visit to Gunung Brinchang, Tea Centre, Butterfly Farm and Strayberry Farm.
Be amazed by the unforgettable view of 2032m above sea level when you visit Cameron Highlands
Visit the famours tea planation factory, tea cafe and tea shop at Sg. Palas Tea Centre
Enjoy the sweet flavor of freshly picked strawberries and delicious desserts with a visit to a local strawberry farm
Get away from the city and have a fascinating nature experience on a half day tour at Cameron Highlands!
Activities:
- See the beauty of Malaysia's wildlife and nature with a short trek around Mossy Forest
- Visit the famed BOH Tea Centre and discover how the delicious tea is produced as you explore the plantation
- Take in a scenic view of Cameron Highlands' lush green natural landscapes
- Visit a tea plantation and enjoy the scenic views of the green tea carpet
- Walk on the forest path and find unique flora and fauna along the way
- Check out how this fresh tea leaf gets turned into a beverage for you to drink

District 21 in IOI City Mall Putrajaya

District21 is a first of its kind, apocalypse themed Adventure Park. This action park is located in a 52,000 sq.ft, indoor premise with a 55ft air-conditioned atrium, within IOI City Mall, Putrajaya. Comprising 8 prominent attractions and 5 function rooms, District21 also offers an ideal venue for corporate team building events, school group visitations and birthday parties. Void of ‘passive’ rides, the credo of District21 is Fun, Passion, and its Participation requires the engagement of the entire family. Incorporating 8 adventure attractions, many of which have never been seen in Asia, District21 offers activities for all visitors to get you climbing, jumping, riding, sliding and flying.
Spend an adrenaline filled day at District 21, and experience it's future post-apocalyptic world designs with your friends and family
Explore all 8 adventure attractions within, filled with climbing, sliding, riding, jumping, free falling and flying
Choose from Easy courses such as the Tubby Ride, Yoyo Car, Launch Pad, Power Station Free Fall
OR the Maze to the Medium difficulty course such as Low Ropes to the Harder courses such as Ninja Warrior, Sky Trail and Roller Glider
Play and stop at your own leisure with this multi-entry ticket

- District 21's Info Counter with it's futuristic design

- Enter "The Maze", a series of twists and turns with puzzles, cages, and a labyrinth within

Explore past Kuala Lumpur's major attractions and discover illusion 3D Art Museum! Spend a day inside the museum, where gigantic 3D art paintings dominate the walls and the floors. Take photos of your friends and family posing in different kinds of scenarios; encounters with zombies and celebrities, dangerous cliffs and deadly accidents, as well as meetings with exotic animals. Pet an elephant by the riverbanks, meet extinct dinosaurs, or be stranded with a hungry polar bear in the middle of the ocean. SKYTREX Adventure Langkawi

Save your beach getaways and sun tans for last and dive head first for an exhilarating outdoor adventure at Skytrex in Langkawi! The amazing team behind Skytrex cooked up the most challenging obstacles for all ages. Each trail showcases different difficulty levels with a range of challenges along the circuits: Eagle Thrill, and Extreme Challenge. Enjoy a fun and relaxed action in the Little Legend Adventure with the many zipline journey from tree to tree and take on a balancing obstacle atop vertical and horizontal bridges in the midst of the jungle. At the Eagle Thrill, dare to conquer height-induced obstacles such as the Flying Fox, Plank Bridge and many more. Not for the faint-hearted, the Island Extreme houses 35 notorious tasks that would make even the biggest thrill-seeker tremble in fear. Participants have to go through the Saddle, Cargo Net Up, X Bridge, Surf Board so it's best to stay hydrated throughout the entire course! The forest's natural and misty environment makes for the suspense of each Skytrex challenge, there's always plenty of room to keep the obstacles interesting. Complete with safety briefing, gear and equipment, and a friendly English speaking instructor, Skytrex Adventure is worth every penny if you're up for an extraordinary physical challenge!
You can easily spend hours of fun with your friends and family
Skytrex Langkawi tops the list of must-visit attractions in the magical island of Langkawi
Great outdoor experience where you can fly, swing, glide and dangle on the various aerial obstacles suspended above the lush tropical Malaysian rainforest
Experience an intense outdoor adventure activity at SKYTREX Adventure when you visit Langkawi!
Challenge yourself and take on different level obstacles from beginner (Little Legend), intermediate (Eagle Thrill) to advanced (Island Extreme)

Upside Down House Gallery Melaka Ticket

Unleash your crazy side with the entire family and friends at Upside Down House Gallery! Modeled after the archetypal Malaysian house, the exhibition is divided into 5 specially designated rooms – living room, kitchen, baby room, master bedroom, and bathroom. All are well-furnished with home décor and interiors, except they're located in their usual places upside down! The main entrance is also a popular place that people visit for fun pictures.Make the most out of your experience by trying different angles in this unique and fun place and be sure to strike some wacky poses! Don't hesitate to ask any member of the staff, they might even suggest some fun ideas for your snaps!
The house consists of a gallery complete with interiors and decors, much like a real house, except that they’re in a completely upside-down position!
If you're up for a good laugh and a great time, this is one of the must-visit family-friendly attractions in Malaysia
Unleash your quirky side and snap yourself some mind-bending photos
Don't forget to book your tickets early – the place gets easily crowded with locals and tourists!
Built by a director with 10-year industry experience, this is a new landmark Melaka is becoming known for

Langkawi Mangrove Tours Package

Discover the enchanting wonders of Langkawi's mangroves with our Mangrove Tours Package. Embark on a captivating journey through lush greenery and winding waterways, immersing yourself in the rich biodiversity of this unique ecosystem.The Langkawi Mangrove Tours Package offers an immersive and educational experience, perfect for those looking to explore Langkawi’s natural beauty and diverse ecosystems. With a mix of adventure, wildlife encounters, and relaxation, this tour promises a memorable day for all participants.
Gain a deeper understanding of local culture and traditions with visits to nearby fishing villages
Experience the thrill of watching majestic eagles swoop down to catch their prey during our exhilarating eagle feeding sessions.
Witness a plethora of wildlife species including exotic birds, monkeys, and elusive otters as they thrive in their natural habitat.
Venture into mysterious limestone caves nestled within the mangroves, unveiling hidden chambers and ancient formations.
Join knowledgeable guides who will lead you through the maze of mangrove forests, offering insights into the diverse flora and fauna.

Bat Cave:Venture into the mysterious Bat Cave, home to thousands of bats. Observe these nocturnal creatures in their natural habitat while learning about their importance to the ecosystem.Fish Farm:Visit a local fish farm to see a variety of marine life up close. Learn about traditional aquaculture practices and have the opportunity to feed some of the fish.Eagle Feeding:Witness the awe-inspiring sight of eagles swooping down to catch their prey. This eagle feeding session offers a thrilling experience and fantastic photo opportunities.Mangrove Sightseeing:Cruise through the intricate network of mangrove forests. Marvel at the diverse flora and fauna, and learn about the crucial role mangroves play in coastal protection and marine life.Crocodile Cave:Navigate through Crocodile Cave, named for its crocodile-shaped rock formations. This natural wonder is accessible during low tide and offers a unique exploration experience.Open Sea (Andaman Sea):Experience the vastness of the Andaman Sea as you move from the serene mangroves to the open waters. Enjoy the breathtaking seascapes and the refreshing sea breeze.Icon Kilim Geoforest Park:Discover the iconic Kilim Geoforest Park, a UNESCO-listed site known for its stunning karst landscapes, rich biodiversity, and geological significance.Tanjung Rhu View:Take in the panoramic views of Tanjung Rhu, one of Langkawi's most beautiful beaches. This spot is perfect for capturing stunning photographs and enjoying the natural scenery.Swimming at the Island Beach:End your tour with a relaxing swim at a secluded island beach. Enjoy the clear, turquoise waters and pristine sands, perfect for unwinding after a day of adventure.
Carry a water bottle to stay hydrated. Light snacks may be provided, but you can bring your own if you have specific dietary needs.
Bring essentials such as sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, swimwear, a towel, and insect repellent. A waterproof bag is recommended to protect your belongings.
Check the weather forecast before the tour. In case of adverse weather, the tour may be rescheduled or canceled for safety reasons.

Lebam River Kayaking Adventure from Desaru , Johor

Enjoy a scenic pick-up from your hotel within the Desaru Coast area followed by a comfortable 15-minute drive to the tranquil Lebam River. Upon arrival, you will board a local transfer boat that transports you and the kayaks upstream to the quieter upper section of the river where the adventure begins. Once on the water, paddle gently through calm mangrove-lined channels surrounded by lush greenery, where you may spot wild ducks, herons, and colorful kingfishers that thrive within this serene ecosystem.After approximately 90 minutes of kayaking, you will meet the safety boat for the return journey back to Kuala Sungai Lebam. Before heading back to your hotel, the tour includes a stop at the popular Desaru Fruit Farm Shop, where you can browse and purchase locally harvested fresh fruits. The experience concludes with a relaxed drive back to your accommodation in Desaru Coast, offering a perfect blend of active exploration and natural beauty.
Enjoy a peaceful paddle on the Lebam River, spotting herons, swallows, monkeys, crabs, and the vibrant mangrove ecosystem.
Explore the ways wildlife and distinctive mangrove trees thrive within Lebam River’s tide-driven ecosystem.
Discover the harmonious relationship between Sungai Lebam’s fishing communities and nature.
Recommended Attire; Hat, t-shirt, shorts or long pants, sun protection, covered shoes or sports shoes, towel, and a change of clothes.
Ensure to bring along a dry bag to store your camera and phone.
Free pick-up from hotels within Desaru.

Rumah Terbalik The Upside Down House of Borneo Ticket in Sabah

Rumah Terbalik is the first upside-down house in Malaysia. This building is situated along the main highway leading to Mount Kinabalu, Kiulu River rafting and all the districts on the east coast of Sabah. Today, you will be able to view a traditional Sabah kampung house fully equipped with kitchen, living room, bedrooms, toilet, veranda and garden in a new perspective – upside down! The upside-down kampung house is designed for all ages, nationalities and it’s wheelchair friendly. As parts of the Tuaran Craft Villages, you’ll also be able to browse in our well stocked gift shop (i.e Tamu). Should you need to answer your nature call, please stop by thier garden toilet! A visit to the upside-down house guarantees to be one of your most memorable experiences and it is a must-see attraction that you should cover in your Sabah itinerary.

Melaka River Cruise

Melaka River Cruise, Malaysia – Through Lights and Colours; Experience Melaka’s rich and vibrant heritage’s focal point – where the trading ships docked and the goods traded. Taking a Melaka River Cruise allows you to experience part of the history that goes back 600 years. Journey through the heart of the UNESCO heritage site giving you an exclusive riverscape view of the city. Best enjoyed in the evening, this river cruise promises a serene and captivating journey to the past.
A Different Perspective & Melaka’s Modern Side
The River That Flowed Through Time
Historical Landmarks of Different Eras

The Melaka River Cruise is certainly a worthwhile trip for your visit to the historic city of Melaka. It is a river that is embedded in history and made this city a success in the first place. Popular with both local and international tourists, the journey is not difficult or strenuous, so it’s something that the whole family can do, from kids to seniors.
Wear light, breathable clothing suitable for warm and humid weather, as the cruise is mostly outdoors.
Opt for comfortable, flat shoes or sandals for easy boarding and disembarkation.
Bring a light jacket or shawl for the evening breeze during night cruises.
This attraction/activity is subject to weather conditions
What’s included: 45 minute Melaka River Cruise

Melaka Wonderland

Melaka Wonderland Theme Park & Resort is located in Ayer Keroh Melaka, one of Malaysia’s most popular destination. Melaka Wonderland is the latest tourist attraction for both local and overseas tourist. It is a complete water park experience for everyone - families, kids, teens and thrill seeker alike. From high-speed water slides to tranquil greenery, the Melaka Wonderland Theme Park & Resort would excite any visitor. This unique water theme park features 11 attractions, namely Tornado Chaser (1st in Malaysia), Lazy cum Crazy River, 1 Big Wave pool, Adventure Island and is surrounded by 360 hectares of natural recreational forest. Melaka Wonderland’s exclusive attractions promise unlimited fun, taking every visitor on a thrilling ride and breathtaking view of a natural forest. Go inside this colorful water ball & start journey walk across the pool.
It's a complete water park experience for families, kids, teens, and thrill seekers!
These attractions include the Tornado Chaser, Kamikaze Racer, 1 Big Wave Pool, Adventure Island, and more!
You can also enjoy lake activities like pedal boat rides as well as rabbit and fish feeding to maximize your fun!
Melaka Wonderland Theme Park is a famous waterpark with 11 exhilarating and water-splashing attractions.
Try the multi-level active water play structure that combines 8 slides into one big playground.

Expect a splashing good time at Melaka Wonderland! Dive into a world of fun with thrilling water slides, a relaxing lazy river, and a wave pool perfect for families. Enjoy the excitement of rafting with friends or simply relax and soak up the sun poolside. Kids and adults alike will love the interactive water play areas, while the park's commitment to cleanliness ensures a safe and enjoyable experience for everyone.
Weather: In the event of severe weather conditions, it may become necessary to temporarily close the pools and attractions.

Restrictions: Height or any restrictions for the slides in the park, please kindly visit Melaka Wonderland Theme Park official website for more information.
Dresscode: Appropriate swimwear is recommended.

Chao Phraya Tourist Boat Bangkok Hop-On-Hop-Off Sightseeing Boat

Chao Phraya Tourist Boat Co., Ltd. provides the original hop-on-hop-off boat service along Chao Phraya River, Bangkok, Thailand. The service has been established since 2003 by Chao Phraya Express Boat Co., Ltd., the leading public water transportation in Bangkok. Chao Phraya Tourist Boat Co., Ltd. established itself on 15th June, 2012 after separating from Chao Phraya Express Boat. Chao Phraya Tourist Boat runs the hop-on-hop-off boat service between 09.00 - 19.15 hrs from Sathorn Pier to Phra Arthit piers and 08.30 - 18.30 hrs from Phra Arthit piers to Sathorn Pier. Boats depart every 30 minutes from 9 piers, including: Sathorn Pier (BTS Saphan Taksin-S6), ICONSIAM Pier, Ratchawongse Pier, Rajinee Pier, Wat Arun Pier, Tha Chang Pier, Tha Maharaj Pier, Prannok Pier, Phra Arthit Pier. From 16.10 to 17.00 hrs, the route is extended to Asiatique as well.
Explore Bangkok at your own pace with our flexible hop-on-hop-off service.
Admire the breathtaking views of Bangkok's skyline and iconic landmarks as you journey along the Chao Phraya River.
Rest assured knowing that our boats operate with punctuality and efficiency.
Travel in comfort aboard our modern and well-equipped boats, featuring spacious seating areas, shaded decks, and onboard amenities.
Our hop-on-hop-off service in Bangkok lets you easily visit famous places like Wat Arun, the Grand Palace, Wat Pho, and bustling Chinatown markets.

Route: The boat travels along the Chao Phraya River, offering scenic views of Bangkok's landmarks like the Grand Palace, Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn), Wat Pho (Temple of the Reclining Buddha), and more.Hop-on, Hop-off Convenience: You can get on and off the boat at any of the designated piers, allowing you to explore various attractions at your own pace.
Accessibility: Toilet: Restroom facilities are accessible for your convenience.
Accessibility: Stroller Accessible; Wheelchair not accessible
What’s included: All Day River Pass

Phuket Fantasea

Inspired by Thailand’s exotic heritage, Phuket FantaSea not only showcases the charm and beauty of Thailand, but also enriches ancient Thai traditions with the wonder of cutting-edge technology and special effects. The result is a stunning 30-acre cultural theme park that has become the ultimate in night-time entertainment on the resort island of Phuket. Created at a cost of over 3,500 million Baht (US$100 million), Phuket FantaSea offers a multitude of attractions not found anywhere else in the world.
Offers a unique entertainment experience that immerses guests in the rich heritage of Thailand.
Spectacular performances to cutting-edge special effects, Phuket FantaSea stands as the ultimate nighttime destination on the island.
Unforgettable journey into the heart of Thai culture and folklore.

Cultural Extravaganza: The highlight is the "Fantasy of a Kingdom" show, featuring Thai cultural performances, acrobatics, illusions, special effects, and elephants (be aware of some controversy surrounding animal shows).Theme Park Ambiance: Explore the park grounds with various entertainment options like carnival games, a shopping village featuring Thai handicrafts, and a simulated Thai palace.Golden Kinnaree Buffet Restaurant: Enjoy a pre-show buffet with a variety of Thai and international dishes (separate ticket required).
Dress Code: There's no strict dress code, but smart casual attire is recommended.
Dress Code:  Comfortable clothing and shoes are ideal as you'll be walking around the park.
Accessibility: The park has wheelchair-accessible pathways for most areas. However, some rides or shows might have limitations
Weather: Phuket has a tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round. The rainy season is typically from May to October

Legong Dance Show Tickets at Ubud Palace Bali

Legong Dance, also known as Legong Kraton, is one of Bali’s most revered classical court dances, once performed exclusively within royal palaces. Originating in the 19th century, it is renowned for its intricate choreography, expressive gestures, and fast-paced movements executed by young female dancers adorned in elaborate gold costumes. Accompanied by a powerful gamelan orchestra, the dance narrates an ancient royal tale of love, conflict, and fate, blending elegance, precision, and storytelling into a mesmerizing cultural performance now enjoyed at cultural stages across Bali.
Experience the essence of Bali's cultural heritage through the visually stunning Legong Dance
Delve into Bali's royal history as you witness the Legong Kraton, originally performed exclusively within palace walls for the royal family
atch in awe as young female performers demonstrate incredible agility and grace, executing the demanding poses and quick movements of the Legong Dance

The highly stylized Legong Kraton enacts a drama of a most purified and abstract kind. The story is performed by three dancers: a female attendant of the court and two identically dressed legongs who adopt the roles of royal persons. The suggestive themes of the magnificent gamelan orchestra and the minds of the audience conjure up imaginary changes of scene. The story derives from the history of East Java in the 12th and 13th centuries. A king finds the maiden Rangkesari lost in the forest. He takes her home and locks her in a house of stone. Rangkesari's brother, the Prince of Daha, learns of her captivity and threatens war unless she is set free. Rangkesari begs her captor to avoid war by giving her liberty, but the king prefers to fight. On his way to battle, he is met by a bird of ill omen that predicts his death. In the fight that ensues he is killed. The dance dramatizes the farewells of the King as he departs for the battlefield and his ominous encounter with the bird.

Melasti Beach Kecak Dance Show

The Kecak dance was created in the 1930s by a Balinese artist named Wayan Limbak and a German painter named Walter Spies. The Balinese Kecak dance is inspired by the Sanghyang dance. Sanghyang dance itself is a sacred Balinese dance that is performed in Hindu temples to drive away misfortune and disease. The original Kecak dance is only performed by men aged 50 years and over.Meanwhile, the name of the Kecak dance is inspired by the word “Cak… cak… cak…” which is chanted throughout the dance performance. In the past, the Balinese Kecak dance was only performed in certain areas, such as Bona Village in Gianyar Regency. Day after day, Kecak is performed in almost all areas of Bali, especially on special occasions to welcome guests. But nowadays, this dance drama is shown everywhere in Bali, especially in culture-based tourist destinations.EXCLUDE:Parking Fee : Motor cycle Rp 3.000,-Car         Rp 5.000,-Bus         Rp 15.000,-Ticket Entry Pantai Melasti For ForeignerAdult Rp 15.000,-Child Rp  3.000,-For IndonesianAdult Rp 10.000,-Child Rp 2.000,-

Immerse yourself in the rich cultural heritage of Bali with captivating Kecak performances held right on the beach.
Dive into a world of excitement with an array of thrilling water sports, from exhilarating surfing adventures to heart-pounding jet ski rides.
Escape the crowds and find your own piece of paradise on Kecak Melasti Beach.
Embark on a picturesque coastal hike that reveals stunning vistas of the sea meeting lush landscapes.

Expect to be captivated by the mesmerizing chants, enchanting dances, and the rich cultural tapestry of Balinese traditions portrayed in the Kecak Melasti Dance—a vibrant and immersive experience showcasing the spiritual and artistic heritage of Bali.

The destination resort integrates wellness, leisure, cultural, residential and commercial real estate supported by infrastructure that adheres to international standards.	Treasure Bay Bintan’s Crystal Lagoon is South-East Asia’s first man-made seawater lagoon at 6.3 hectares. Encore Melaka  Admission Ticket

Encore Melaka, the brainchild of Datuk Wira Boo Kuang Loon, founder and CEO of Yong Tai Berhad. The planning of this theatre began in 2013 and the first performance was held on 7 July 2018. This award-winning architectural marvel, the first of its kind in Southeast Asia, features a unique 360-degree rotating auditorium with a seating capacity of 1,800 and a 240-meter-long stage equipped with advanced technology, including water, mist, multi-lifting, and multi-storey stages. The vision behind Encore Melaka is to blend tradition with innovation, offering dynamic, immersive performances that creatively present Melaka’s rich heritage. Strategically located near Melaka’s UNESCO World Heritage Sites, this premier cultural hub aims to elevate Melaka’s status as a global tourist destination, fostering economic growth and creating job opportunities for the local community.

ENCORE MELAKA is an immersive performance with a series of untold stories of Melaka, which reflects a society that embraces diversity and inclusiveness. This 70-minutes long mega show reminiscing life stories of the locals with state-of-the-art production sets. The audience will experience 360-degree rotating audience platform fronting a 240-meter long stage, multi-lifting platforms, sound & light and a 3D mapping projection that creates virtual reality that travels through time. Encore Melaka Theatre is a multi-venue performing arts center in Melaka City.This award-winning architectural venue where it continuously develops to be a vibrant performing arts theatre in Malaysia, providing a venue that continues to inspire and revitalize your life with the best experiences. Encore Melaka is able to accommodate a wide range of events including events, performances, product launching, movie premiers, film shoots, award ceremonies, seminars, exhibitions, meetings, and other corporate events.

Terms & Conditions: Some rides have specific height and weight limits for safety reasons. Children under 120cm tall must be accompanied by an adult who is at least 16 years old. Children aged below 1 year are to be FOC with a paying adult. FREE park admission applies only to children aged below 1 year with a paying adult. (i.e., if the children turn 1 in 2025, a ticket is required for the visitation date from 1 January 2025 to 31 December 2025) People aged 17 and older will pay the same price as adults. A child is considered anyone from 1 to 16 years old. Please review the Fun Park's rules and regulations for more information. Guests in wheelchairs may need to transfer to the ride vehicle seat. Guests cannot bring outside food or drinks into the park. Dangerous items like bottles, firecrackers, and lasers are not allowed. Only service animals with proper identification are allowed. Smoking and drinking are prohibited in the park. If caught, you may be asked to leave.General Park Rules & Regulations Follow Ride Instructions: 1. Always follow the instructions given by ride operators, including how to board and exit the ride/attraction, as well as safety guidelines. Ensure to always keep all body parts inside the ride/attraction. Height, Weight and Age Restrictions: 2. Amusement parks have rides with different classes of height, weight, and age restrictions for safety reasons. Make sure to adhere to these restrictions to ensure a safe and enjoyable experience. Secure Loose Items: 3. Secure loose items such as hats, sunglasses, and cell phones before getting on a ride/attraction to prevent them from falling and causing injury or damage. Dress Code: 4. Dress appropriately and avoid clothing with loose straps or dangling accessories that could get caught in rides/attractions. Stay Hydrated: 5. Drink plenty of water to stay hydrated, especially on hot days. No Smoking & Vaping: 6. Smoking and Vaping is prohibited in ride queues, on rides, or other non-designated areas. No Alcohol or Drugs: 7. Alcohol and drugs are prohibited to maintain a safe and family-friendly environment. Respect Queues: 8. Don't cut queues. Wait your turn and be courteous to others. Use Trash Bins: 9. Dispose of trash in designated bins and help keep the park clean. Supervise Children: 10. If you have young children with you, always keep an eye on them and make sure they follow the rules and safety guidelines. Listen to Announcements: 11. Pay attention to announcements over the public address system for important information or changes in park operations. Photo and Video Policies: 12. Be aware of the park's photography and video recording policies, especially on rides. Respect the privacy of others when taking photos or videos
Cancellation Policy: No cancellation for this activity as per vendor policy

Singapore Tourist Pass – 1/2/3/4/5 Day

The Singapore Tourist Pass is a special EZ-Link card thatoffers tourist unlimited travel* on Singapore’s basic bus services, MRT and LRT trains for the duration that it isvalid.You can now take in the sights and sounds of Singapore inthe comfort of the island’s extensive train system and public bus network. Atwallet-friendly prices, you do not have to worry about having insufficientfunds for your transport needs with the Singapore Tourist Pass.The Singapore Tourist Pass is availablein 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5-Day, depending on the duration of your stay in Singapore!*T&Cs:The duration and validity of the pass is based on the operating hours of the basic public transport system, 1-day coverage of the pass begins from your 1stusage of the pass to the end of the public transport service for that day. For timings and operational hours of the bus services, please refer to SimplyGo’s website at:https://www.simplygo.com.sg/travel-guideUsage of 2-day, 3-day, 4-day, or 5-day card must be on consecutive days.Unlimited travel is only applicable for public bus and train services. The pass excludes rides on premium services such as Sentosa Express, RWS8, express buses as well as other premium and niche bus services.To use these premium services, you can add value to the card via the self-service ticketing machines or at the SimplyGo Ticket Office.Prices apply to adult and child above 0.9m in height.The 1/2/3/4/5-Day pass has a validity of 1 year from date of purchase, while the card has a validity period of 5 years from date of purchase.The Singapore Tourist Pass is non-refundable and non-returnable.One (1) Singapore Tourist Pass can only be used by one (1) person at a time.You are required to redeem the Singapore Tourist Pass within 90 days from the booking confirmation date.A QR Code will be sent in the confirmation email.For redemption, please present the QR Code to the staff at the selected ticket office or scan the QR Code at our automated STP kiosks.
Flash your STP Card to enjoy perks and discounts on food and beverages, attractions, entertainment, local tours, retail, and more!
Travel worry-free, no credit card transaction fees
Spare the hassle of topping up, simply tap and go!
Redeem upon arrival and start using it immediately
